bio: I've always had a natural interest in technology and understanding how complex systems work, especially in areas like IT, cybersecurity, and aviation. I enjoy figuring things out, whether that's solving a technical problem or learning something completely new.

Alongside that, I'm really into running and fitness. I've trained for longer distances like half marathons, and I enjoy the challenge of improving my endurance and staying consistent. It's something that keeps me focused and balanced outside of everything else.

In my free time, I like exploring new ideas, keeping up with developments in tech, and working on personal projects. This website is a space where I can share those interests, track my progress, and put some of what I'm learning into practice.

education.description: Brief description — e.g. modules studied, grade achieved, notable projects.

Pass Overall - 3 A-Level Equivalent  

Digital T-Level course is for students who wish to progress into skilled IT employment or higher-level technical training or study.

The main area of study covers:

Digital Software Development
Employer Set Project
Occupational Specialism Project
Weekly industry placement within the industry
T-Level has been developed in collaboration with employers so the content meets the needs of industry, preparing students for the workplace.

Mixture of classroom-based teaching and learning and ‘on-the-job’ learning as the course involves at least 315 hours (approximately 45 days) of Industrial Placement.

This new qualification provides the essential knowledge, understanding and skills relevant to any digital occupation.
